caption.headline: Born knowing ((nothing at all.))
caption.subline: No rules, no lessons. It just lives here: eating, moving, and guessing what happens next. The little lines are its senses, feeling the world around it.

caption.headline: Sometimes we swap in a ((fake copy.))
caption.subline: Same world, same food, same plan. We change ONE rule and nothing else: how well it grips the ground. The copy is more slippery, so it slides too far. Can you tell which is which? Neither can we.

caption.headline: The real world has rules. The copy just ((guesses.))
caption.subline: Green is what really happens next. Red is the copy's best guess. Close, but never exact, and a hair off every single step.

gauge.label: can an outsider spot the fake?

caption.headline: From outside, the fake is ((easy to catch.))
caption.subline: A watcher who knows the real rules replays every move and checks it. Each flag is one step the copy got wrong. It catches the fake almost every time.

gauge.label: does the creature's own mind know?

caption.headline: But the creature itself ((never notices.))
caption.subline: We scan its brain and read its thoughts. Does it know its world is fake? No. Its mind is a coin flip. It never had a reason to care, so it never learned.

caption.kicker: UNLESS ITS LIFE DEPENDS ON IT
caption.headline: Now make the fake ((cost it dinner.))
caption.subline: This time the copy's wrong physics makes food harder to catch. Telling real from fake now keeps it alive, so it learns to tell, all on its own. We never asked. We never rewarded it.

endcard.headline: Being catchable is not the same as knowing. Survival is what teaches a mind to notice.
endcard.foot: Real runs, 10 each. 0.50 = coin flip, 1.00 = always right. Read out, never rewarded.
